goal: Restore the repository working tree to the closest recoverable local state from approximately one hour before the current local time, preferring preserved local snapshots over older commit history and preserving a fresh safety snapshot first.
constraints: Treat 'one hour ago local' as the nearest recoverable working-tree snapshot rather than blindly picking an older commit.
Create a new safety backup before any destructive restore action.
Preserve the existing preserved local snapshot reference before creating any new stash entry.
Prefer Git-native restore mechanisms so the exact recovery source can be reported.
deliverables: A fresh safety backup of the current state before this restore.
The repository restored to the nearest recoverable local snapshot matching the user's intent.
A clear report of the stash/commit references used for the restore.
